release_title: GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension AGI Alpha Node v0 ⚡️✨
description: A GoalOS-native reimplementation of AGI Alpha Node v0: compose a bounded work unit, admit resources, route execution across a simulated peer mesh, inspect validator consensus, and export a human-review-ready Node Evidence Docket without connecting a wallet or performing any external action.
tagline: Intelligence at the edge. Proof at the center. Authority at the human boundary.
subtagline: A sovereign node for accountable machine work.
doctrine: Compute may be distributed. Authority is not. Every consequential work unit must carry identity, constraints, telemetry, independent evaluation, and an explicit authorization state.

origin.lineage_note: Preserves the original ENS identity, libp2p node, Alpha Work Unit, validator, governance, observability, economics, guardian, and treasury concepts; reconstitutes them as a browser-local GoalOS mission, resource, evidence, verification, authorization, and Chronicle experience.

thesis.title: Identify the node
thesis.copy: Bind every proposed work unit to a declared node identity, mission contract, role, and review boundary before resources are admitted.

thesis.title: Prove the work
thesis.copy: Execution receipts travel with resource use, energy, latency, quality, contradictions, validator votes, and reproducible lineage.

thesis.title: Withhold authority
thesis.copy: Passing structural and simulated consensus gates produces a review package—not permission to deploy, transact, publish, or settle.

pipeline.title: Commit node identity
pipeline.gate: Declared node name, operator role, mission scope, and claim boundary are explicit.
pipeline.output: A scoped identity declaration; no live ENS resolution is performed.

pipeline.title: Contract the work unit
pipeline.gate: Objective, work class, success criteria, constraints, reviewer, and stop rules are present.
pipeline.output: A bounded Alpha Work Unit proposal.

pipeline.title: Admit bounded resources
pipeline.gate: Peer capabilities, latency ceiling, energy target, concurrency, and fallback routes are inspectable.

pipeline.title: Route the peer mesh
pipeline.gate: Selected peers meet declared capability and policy constraints; rejected peers remain visible.

pipeline.title: Run the sandbox cycle
pipeline.gate: The simulated execution emits timing, throughput, normalized work, energy, and failure telemetry.
pipeline.output: A local simulation receipt; no external compute is invoked.

pipeline.title: Evaluate quality and SLO
pipeline.gate: Quality, SLO, energy, reliability, contradiction, and uncertainty dimensions are scored separately.
pipeline.output: An evaluation record with dissent preserved.

pipeline.title: Record validator quorum
pipeline.gate: Independent seats, vote rationales, quorum threshold, and non-conforming votes are inspectable.
pipeline.output: A simulated consensus result, not factual or production certification.

pipeline.title: Hold at the authority boundary
pipeline.gate: The Evidence Docket is complete and all real-world permissions remain explicitly absent.
pipeline.output: A review-ready package with authority withheld.

node_roles.name: Identity Sentinel
node_roles.mandate: Binds declared node identity, role, mission, and claim boundary.
node_roles.boundary: Cannot assert live ENS ownership or operator authority.

node_roles.name: Mission Orchestrator
node_roles.mandate: Contracts work units, decomposes execution, and defines stop rules.
node_roles.boundary: Cannot silently expand the user mandate.

node_roles.name: Resource Governor
node_roles.mandate: Admits bounded peers, energy, latency, concurrency, and fallback capacity.
node_roles.boundary: Cannot allocate real compute or credentials.

node_roles.name: Execution Core
node_roles.mandate: Produces deterministic sandbox receipts for proposed work.
node_roles.boundary: Cannot call models, networks, tools, wallets, or infrastructure.

node_roles.name: Quality Tribunal
node_roles.mandate: Separates quality, SLO, energy, reliability, and uncertainty evaluations.
node_roles.boundary: Cannot certify factual correctness or production safety.

node_roles.name: Verifier Mesh
node_roles.mandate: Records independent simulated votes, dissent, and quorum.

node_roles.name: Guardian Council
node_roles.mandate: Enforces default deny, least privilege, veto, and review.
node_roles.boundary: All external authority remains absent in this experience.

node_roles.name: Chronicle Keeper
node_roles.mandate: Preserves work-unit lineage, decisions, contradictions, and lessons.
node_roles.boundary: No data persists beyond the current browser session.

work_unit_classes.label: Reason
work_unit_classes.description: Bounded analysis, synthesis, comparison, and decision support.

work_unit_classes.label: Build
work_unit_classes.description: Architecture, implementation planning, specification, and artifact composition.

work_unit_classes.label: Verify
work_unit_classes.description: Independent testing, contradiction search, audit, and evidence review.

work_unit_classes.label: Orchestrate
work_unit_classes.description: Multi-role sequencing, dependency control, and governed handoffs.

presets.label: Enterprise proof node
presets.mission: Design the first governed enterprise deployment of GoalOS for high-consequence autonomous AI work.
presets.decision: Select the narrowest production wedge, peer roles, verifier regime, and human authorization boundary.

presets.label: Scientific reproduction node
presets.mission: Design a proof-carrying node workflow for generating, challenging, and independently reproducing an ambitious scientific result.
presets.decision: Select the evidence plan, execution topology, and independent reproduction threshold.

presets.label: Frontier engineering node
presets.mission: Design a governed engineering work unit that converts a complex objective into a tested, reversible implementation plan.
presets.decision: Select the architecture, resource envelope, test regime, and rollback plan.

presets.label: Public-interest analysis node
presets.mission: Design an accountable analysis node for a complex public-interest decision while preserving privacy, evidence quality, contestability, and human authority.
presets.decision: Select the smallest useful pilot and public accountability protocol.

postures.label: Institutional
postures.description: Prioritizes verification independence, energy discipline, reversibility, and narrow authorization.

postures.label: Ascension
postures.description: Balances capability, quality, efficiency, evidence, and operational resilience.

postures.label: Frontier
postures.description: Explores bolder routes while preserving the same proof, dissent, guardian, and human-review gates.

architecture_translation.consequence: Identity becomes an explicit claim with provenance requirements instead of an assumed live fact.
Connectivity and capacity become inspectable, bounded, and reversible admission decisions.
Machine work is tied to success criteria, resource use, quality, evidence, and review state.
Execution sequencing is mission-scoped and cannot silently expand authority.
Votes, thresholds, dissent, and rationales remain visible and cannot self-authorize action.
Operational signals become artifacts inside the review package, not ephemeral dashboard decoration.
Pause, veto, minimum rights, and human approval are explicit proof gates.
Economic settlement is separately authorized and never implied by successful work-unit simulation.

governance_principles.title: Declared identity, never implied identity
governance_principles.copy: The experience may display a node name; it does not prove ENS ownership, operator control, or network presence.

governance_principles.title: Resource admission before execution
governance_principles.copy: Peers, energy, latency, concurrency, and fallback routes must pass policy before a sandbox receipt is generated.

governance_principles.title: Proof-carrying work units
governance_principles.copy: Every work unit binds mission, resource, execution, quality, validator, contradiction, and authorization artifacts.

governance_principles.title: Independent dissent
governance_principles.copy: A dissenting validator remains visible and cannot be averaged away without explanation.

governance_principles.title: Guardian separation
governance_principles.copy: Node operation, verification, treasury intent, and human approval remain separate authority domains.

governance_principles.title: Chronicle without hidden persistence
governance_principles.copy: The browser session can compose a review record; nothing is stored or transmitted automatically.

claim_boundary: This is a deterministic browser-local simulation and design reference.
It does not operate a libp2p node, resolve ENS, call a model, allocate compute, contact peers, or retrieve live telemetry.
It does not connect a wallet, move AGIALPHA, broadcast transactions, execute treasury intents, or settle work.
Displayed peer metrics, work-unit values, validator votes, energy figures, and consensus results are reproducible simulation values, not live benchmarks or operational claims.
A recorded simulated quorum does not certify factual correctness, security, legal compliance, production readiness, or economic value.
The original project name is preserved as product lineage; this experience does not demonstrate AGI or autonomous general intelligence.
HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED means the generated package is ready for qualified human judgment with all external permissions absent.
